summary: Pigpen‑heavy show with epic Good Lovin’ and soaring Bird Song
blurb: The Princeton gym set is a Pigpen‑driven romp highlighted by a monster 27‑minute Good Lovin' (complete with the Brooklyn Bridge rap) and a soaring Bird Song. Jerry’s guitar is explosive on Hard To Handle and the band locks into tight, dance‑able jams throughout both sets.
review: On April 17, 1971, the Grateful Dead turned Dillon Gym into a showcase for Ron “Pigpen” McKernan’s raw charisma and soulful organ work. From the opening Truckin' through a raucous Big Railroad Blues and an uplifting Bird Song, the first set rides a steady wave of energy, punctuated by Jerry Garcia’s powerful guitar on Hard To Handle and a heartfelt Casey Jones. The second set erupts with an unforgettable Good Lovin' that stretches past 27 minutes, complete with Pigpen’s legendary Brooklyn Bridge rap, before rolling into soulful renditions of Me And Bobby McGee, Deal, and a jubilant Bertha. Across all available tapes the band sounds tight—Bill Kreutzmann sits “in the pocket,” while Phil provides a solid bass foundation and Bob Weir adds energetic vocal flair on Casey Jones. This show stands out as one of Pigpen’s finest moments in the early ’70s, delivering both high‑octane rockers and heartfelt blues with a clarity that only the best soundboard captures can reveal.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive; reviewers consistently praise the epic Good Lovin', Pigpen’s organ/vocals, Jerry’s explosive guitar, and tight band interplay. No negative comments about performance appear.

best_recording.reason: Excellent, pristine soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix; preserves the full flow of the set without disruptive track splits.

key_highlights: Epic 27‑minute Good Lovin' featuring Pigpen’s famous Brooklyn Bridge rap
Soaring Bird Song that showcases Jerry’s explosive guitar work
Tight, danceable jams on Hard To Handle and Truckin'
Bill Kreutzmann’s drums sit perfectly in the pocket, giving the set a solid rhythmic foundation

band_performance.Jerry: Explosive guitar tone and powerful solos on Hard To Handle, Bird Song, and Good Lovin'; delivers strong vocal performance despite pre‑show hassles.
band_performance.Phil: Steady bass foundation that underpins the high‑energy Pigpen set; noted as his favorite Pigpen show by a reviewer.
band_performance.Bob: Energetic vocals on Casey Jones and other numbers, adding excitement to the band’s delivery.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are ‘in the pocket,’ providing an intense yet relaxed groove that drives the jams forward.
